text.title: Continuous Discovery Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Continuous Discovery. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: ≥1 user touch per week; missed weeks tracked.
rule.rationale: ≥1 user touch per week; missed weeks tracked.

rule.statement: Assumption tree updated within 24h of each touch.
rule.rationale: Assumption tree updated within 24h of each touch.

rule.statement: Interview / survey / observation rotate; single-mode = bias.
rule.rationale: Interview / survey / observation rotate; single-mode = bias.

rule.statement: Tree update flags ≥1 experiment-worthy assumption per month.
rule.rationale: Tree update flags ≥1 experiment-worthy assumption per month.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-continuous-discovery.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
